parts.text: "Project @OCR4all/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @OCR4all/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/jackson-databind-2.13.4.1-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-jackson-databind-2.13.4.1/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @OCR4all/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

I might need to use globbing and explore a bit. I should provide some progress commentary as I go along!
I’m locating the affected project and the Spoon template, then I’ll inspect the compilation failure and dependency API change before writing a generic transformation.

I'm noticing that a compile error is popping up when I'm trying to use databind methods that are missing an exception class. I wonder if using methods like `writeValue(JsonGenerator, Object)` or `ObjectMapper.writeValueAsString` causes the issue. I need to check if those method signatures include `StreamWriteException`. A search showed that some signatures like `ObjectMapper.writeValue(File, Object)` don’t, but `writeValueAsString` might still throw `JsonProcessingException`, which could be fine since the project involves writing to a file.

I found the key constraint: the break is exposed by `ObjectWriter.writeValue(File, Object)` pulling in newer Jackson core exception types. I’m checking the overloads that remain binary-safe so the generic rewrite can avoid the bad signature across any project.

However, I'm noticing a mismatch with the expected task regarding type parameters. I’m wondering if the clause may include StreamWriteException in databind 2.13? I need to take a look at the ObjectMapper's writeValue signatures.
